titleEn: USask secures funding for advanced poultry research facility
titleZh: 萨斯喀彻温大学获资建设先进家禽研究设施
summaryZh: 萨斯喀彻温大学（USask）获得加拿大创新基金会（CFI）620万加元及行业合作伙伴400万加元资助，建设一座前沿蛋鸡研究设施。该设施将用于研究光照、饲养系统、饲料等对家禽福利和生产性能的影响，并帮助加拿大蛋鸡产业在2036年前淘汰传统笼养系统。项目由家禽福利专家Karen Schwean-Lardner教授主导，旨在通过数据驱动实验推动家禽研究与产业升级。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了加拿大在蛋鸡福利研究基础设施上的重大投资，涉及光照、饲养系统等自动化环境控制方向，与智慧畜牧中数据驱动养殖和福利优化高度相关，值得关注家禽智能化转型的从业者阅读。
contentFull: Equipment & innovation Digital innovation Research and innovation USask secures funding for advanced poultry research facility New unit to boost welfare research, data and housing systems 23 March 2026 3 minute read By: Global Ag Media North America As a leading poultry researcher at the University of Saskatchewan (USask), Karen Schwean-Lardner explores improved light, housing, and feed systems for better welfare, healthier chicken, and egg production in Canada — and she’s not afraid to feed the wild chickens she meets on holiday in Hawaii, according to an article published by the university. And nobody is more excited than Schwean-Lardner about what a new state-of-the-art poultry laying facility at USask would mean for her research field — and her birds.“This will move us so far forward in poultry research,” she said. “This is causing me to push back my retirement because I want the first experiment in a system like this. This is so exciting.”A proposed, cutting-edge poultry laying facility has received more than $6.2 million from the Canada Foundation for Innovation (CFI) Innovation Fund, which supports developing infrastructure to further world-leading research in Canada. In addition, $3 million has been contributed to the new facility by Saskatchewan Egg Producers, an independent farmer-run organization of egg producers in the province, and an additional $1 million has been contributed by USask’s College of Agriculture and Bioresources.“The development of this new facility positions USask to continue as a Canadian and world leader in poultry research and animal welfare,” said USask vice-president of research Baljit Singh. “This funding is a tremendous show of support and vote of confidence from the government and our industry partners for the excellent agricultural research that takes place at USask.”Schwean-Lardner is a professor in USask’s Department of Animal and Poultry Science in the College of Agriculture and Bioresources and the lead researcher on the project. She and Dr. Deborah Adewole (PhD), an assistant professor and poultry expert in the same department, are excited for the opportunity to set a new standard in Canadian poultry research with these new facilities.As part of new guidelines set out by Egg Farmers Canada, “conventional” housing systems for chickens will need to be phased out for all Canadian producers by 2036. Schwean-Lardner and Adewole both said the proposed new facilities would put USask well ahead of the curve and help researchers and industry leaders stay on the cutting edge of poultry and egg production research.“This project makes us commercially relevant for our research, regardless of which system our producers move to,” Schwean-Lardner said. “We can do the research for any producer in Canada, and that’s so cool.”Using these new tools and facilities, researchers at USask will be able to precisely experiment with different types of feed and environments for chickens at an advanced scale, giving new data-driven insights to producers to enhance best practices for poultry welfare and egg production.The blueprints for the proposed new facility emphasize precision and control for researchers, and comfort for the laying hens which will be housed there. Schwean-Lardner said the new, nearly 24,000-square foot facility they hope to build on the USask campus will utilize three types of housing systems for hens: enriched housing, free run, and free-range systems.The enriched housing systems are designed to promote living conditions and reduce aggression levels for chickens. Ten individual housing rooms will be equipped with lighting, temperature, and other environmental variable controls to let the researchers conduct experiments safely and ethically, and the free-range systems will allow the birds to wander the Prairie outdoors.“Big data is getting more attention these days. We have a lot of data that can be gathered over time, and this unit is going to allow us to manage that data and collect it over a long period of time,” Adewole said. “There are going to be a lot of new things that we can do for poultry research.”In addition, special viewing rooms will be available for public groups — from board meetings to schoolchildren — to see the chickens living at the facility. These viewing areas will allow consumers to directly see how housing systems affect birds, without risking biosecurity on farms.“We can control the environment. Right now, we cannot do that in the same way,” Adewole said. “This facility is one of its kind in Canada. There are other universities that have built new facilities, but this one is encompassing all systems and has space for public viewing systems as well — which is a first in Canada.”USask has long been a pillar of agricultural research in Saskatchewan, across Canada, and around the world. Schwean-Lardner said the support of the CFI and Saskatchewan Egg Producers was a tremendous step towards making the new facility a reality.As Schwean-Larder puts it, this facility has the potential to be a USask research cornerstone. “This will be a relevant research facility for the next 50 years. It’s huge, huge, huge,” she said Our Partners World Poultry Science Association Congress Poultry Science Association Targan dsm-firmenich Lallemand Lanxess BioZyme Hy-Line International Global Ag Media More from this author More News View all US to impose 25% tariff on most Brazilian imports from July 22 Markets & policy Market trends Markets and economics 16 July 2026 2 minute read China's chicken exports surge 51% in early 2026 Markets & policy Market trends Markets and economics 16 July 2026 1 minute read Breast meat leads as producers target Japan and Middle East China's chicken consumption to rise on foodservice growth Markets & policy Market trends 16 July 2026 1 minute read White broiler leads as younger consumers favour convenience Our Partners World Poultry Science Association Congress Poultry Science Association Targan dsm-firmenich Lallemand Lanxess BioZyme Hy-Line International
translationZh:
作为萨斯喀彻温大学（USask）的家禽研究领军人物，Karen Schwean-Lardner致力于探索改进的光照、饲养和饲料系统，以提升加拿大肉鸡和蛋鸡的福利、健康及产量——据该校发布的一篇文章称，她在夏威夷度假时也不忘喂食遇到的野鸡。而没有人比Schwean-Lardner更期待USask新建的先进蛋鸡设施能为她的研究领域——以及她的鸡——带来什么。

“这将使我们在家禽研究领域大步向前，”她说。“这让我推迟了退休计划，因为我希望能在这样的系统中进行第一个实验。这太令人兴奋了。”

拟建的尖端蛋鸡设施已从加拿大创新基金会（CFI）创新基金获得超过620万加元资助，该基金旨在支持加拿大世界领先研究所需的基础设施建设。此外，萨斯喀彻温省蛋鸡生产者协会（一个由该省蛋农独立运营的组织）为该新设施贡献了300万加元，USask农业与生物资源学院也额外提供了100万加元。

“这一新设施的开发将使USask继续成为加拿大乃至全球家禽研究与动物福利领域的领导者，”USask研究副校长Baljit Singh表示。“这笔资助是政府和行业合作伙伴对我们优秀农业研究的巨大支持与信任。”

Schwean-Lardner是USask农业与生物资源学院动物与家禽科学系的教授，也是该项目的首席研究员。她与同系的助理教授、家禽专家Deborah Adewole博士都对有机会通过这些新设施为加拿大家禽研究树立新标准感到兴奋。

根据加拿大蛋农协会制定的新指南，到2036年，所有加拿大生产者必须淘汰“传统”鸡舍系统。Schwean-Lardner和Adewole均表示，拟建的新设施将使USask远远领先于行业步伐，帮助研究人员和行业领袖始终处于家禽与蛋品生产研究的前沿。

“这个项目使我们的研究具有商业相关性，无论我们的生产者转向哪种系统，”Schwean-Lardner说。“我们可以为加拿大的任何生产者开展研究，这太酷了。”

借助这些新工具和设施，USask的研究人员将能够在先进规模上精确试验不同类型的饲料和环境，从而获得新的数据驱动成果。
contentHtml:
USask secures funding for advanced poultry research facility 
New unit to boost welfare research, data and housing systems
